title: Reflogs, Reachability Windows, and Recovery · Repository Repair Bay
summary: A responder inherits a plausible but defective reflog-led recovery timeline and retained ref; preserve reachable history, isolate the first causal Git-state mismatch, and retain a regression probe. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in reflog-led recovery timeline and retained ref and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain per-ref and HEAD reflogs, selector syntax, creation policy, worktree scope, expiration categories, and configuration.
Correlate ref updates, checkout history, operation messages, timestamps, actors, and graph evidence without treating reflog order as commit ancestry.
Discover lost tips with reflog, fsck, object inspection, and known content while avoiding commands that may expire or prune candidates.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
debug case — Case G79 is a debug in a disposable repository. the lost topic tip remains named by a local reflog entry inside the expiration window. The learner must predict the timeline reveals the prior topic ID and force-update operation independently of ancestry, run git reflog show --date=iso --all, explain topic@{1} names b00b1e5 before the forced update and its tree matches the missing feature, test this changed condition: expire the topic name in the scenario and recover the candidate through fsck content evidence, and preserve this recovery boundary: create refs/recovery/lost-topic at b00b1e5 before inspecting or integrating it.

Inspect the Gita Reflog Scope Lifetime trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for explain per-ref and head reflogs, selector syntax, creation policy, worktree scope, expiration categories, and configuration.

content.text: For Gita Reflog Scope Lifetime, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the Gita Reflog Scope Lifetime precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Case G79 is a debug in a disposable repository. the lost topic tip remains named by a local reflog entry inside the expiration window. The learner must predict the timeline reveals the prior topic ID and force-update operation independently of ancestry, run git reflog show --date=iso --all, explain topic@{1} names b00b1e5 before the forced update and its tree matches the missing feature, test this changed condition: expire the topic name in the scenario and recover the candidate through fsck content evidence, and preserve this recovery boundary: create refs/recovery/lost-topic at b00b1e5 before inspecting or integrating it.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Reflogs are shared remotely and provide a permanent repository audit log.

stimulus.title: Gita Reflog Scope Lifetime case record

The learner must predict the timeline reveals the prior topic ID and force-update operation independently of ancestry, run git reflog show --date=iso --all, explain topic@{1} names b00b1e5 before the forced update and its tree matches the missing feature, test this changed condition: expire the topic name in the scenario and recover the candidate through fsck content evidence, and preserve this recovery boundary: create refs/recovery/lost-topic at b00b1e5 before inspecting or integrating it.

lines.label: evidence

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Gita Reflog Scope Lifetime, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-expiration-retention-policy-1sr5uj" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Expiration Retention Policy into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-expiration-retention-policy-1sr5uj
# scenario-key: trace-1sr5uj probe-ju5rs1 boundary-r5uj1s owner-1sr5ujx fault-1sr5ujz
# before-ref: main=a11ce1s candidate=b00b1uj
# hypothesis: the lost topic tip remains named by a local reflog entry inside the expiration window
git reflog show --date=iso --all
# expected: the timeline reveals the prior topic ID and force-update operation independently of ancestry
# observation: topic@{1} names b00b1e5 before the forced update and its tree matches the missing feature
# changed-case: expire the topic name in the scenario and recover the candidate through fsck content evidence
# after-ref: main=a11ce1s candidate=c0ffeej
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: create refs/recovery/lost-topic at b00b1e5 before inspecting or integrating it
# transfer-limit: reflogs are local retention aids, not shared audit logs or backup guarantees
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

hints: Name the Gita Expiration Retention Policy precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Case G80 is a debug in a disposable repository. the lost topic tip remains named by a local reflog entry inside the expiration window. The learner must predict the timeline reveals the prior topic ID and force-update operation independently of ancestry, run git reflog show --date=iso --all, explain topic@{1} names b00b1e5 before the forced update and its tree matches the missing feature, test this changed condition: expire the topic name in the scenario and recover the candidate through fsck content evidence, and preserve this recovery boundary: create refs/recovery/lost-topic at b00b1e5 before inspecting or integrating it.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Increasing reflog expiry converts local Git storage into a verified backup.

options.text: Inspect repository boundary, config origin, current operation, worktree, index, refs, and graph
Preserve uncommitted work and a recovery ref, then state intended tree and topology
Preview and perform the least destructive simulated Git operation
Verify status, staged and range diffs, graph, tests, remote target, and recovery path

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write the debug evidence note for Gita Expiration Retention Policy: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Case G87 is a debug in a disposable repository. the lost topic tip remains named by a local reflog entry inside the expiration window. The learner must predict the timeline reveals the prior topic ID and force-update operation independently of ancestry, run git reflog show --date=iso --all, explain topic@{1} names b00b1e5 before the forced update and its tree matches the missing feature, test this changed condition: expire the topic name in the scenario and recover the candidate through fsck content evidence, and preserve this recovery boundary: create refs/recovery/lost-topic at b00b1e5 before inspecting or integrating it.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Design reflog expiration, gc grace, backup, and incident policy from collaboration and compliance needs without promising archival durability.
